steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage describes a study of Serbian consumers, and the researchers warned against applying findings to customers generally. The blank must complete a suggestion explaining why generalization is risky and what should be done instead.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'online retailers that operate in a variety of countries are more likely to increase customer loyalty if they make their deliveries in less time...': This applies the findings broadly to other countries, which is exactly what the researchers cautioned against. ✗
  • Choice B — 'additional research is needed with participants from countries of varying level of economic development to determine whether delivery time and tracking are more important than package design to customers broadly.': This directly addresses the limitation (one economic context, Serbia) by calling for broader research before generalizing. ✓
  • Choice C — 'the greater importance assigned to delivery time and tracking than to package design may not be observed if the survey were to be given to a larger group of Serbian participants.': This addresses sample size within Serbia, not the cross-country generalization problem the researchers flagged. ✗
  • Choice D — 'customers in countries with higher levels of economic development than that of Serbia are likely to regard package design as relatively more important...': This speculates about other countries without supporting evidence, contradicting the caution against generalization. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
